facts.place: Cedar Lake Cemetery, Trimont, Martin, Minnesota, United States
facts.standard_place: Cedar Lake Cemetery, Trimont, Martin, Minnesota, United States

facts.value:
Mr. and Mrs. Samuel Clark were among the first settlers in Martin County, Minnesota. Samuel was born in Coshocton County, Ohio, January 20, 1821, of Irish-German descent, lived and grew to manhood in his home county. He married Meloina Hall on April 25, 1841. She was born May 31, 1825 in Licking County, Ohio of German-American parentage.
   in 1842 they moved to Illinois and remained until 1844 when they moved back to Ohio. in 1846 they moved to Wisconsin living in Greene County for three years. they moved to Winneshiek County, Iowa in 1849. from there they moved to Freeborn County, Minnesota in 1857. In the fall of 1867, they came to Cedar Lake, Martin County, Minnesota.
   Mr. Clark carried the mail from Albert Lea to Blue Earth when they lived in Freeborn County. He was a member of the 10th Minnesota Infantry, General Bull’s campaign in the Dakotas and the pioneer mail carrier of southern Minnesota, thus worthy of mention in our county history.
   They farmed and later owned and operated a hotel in Cedarville, and later in Mountain Lake until 1881.
   Samuel and Meloina had 14 children. Daughter Martha married James K Mason of Winnebago, Minnesota.
Meloina Clark died August 3, 1900. Samuel died June 8th, 1904. Two of their children had died while yet children in 1861. Daughter Seena Stevens had also died prior to the elder Clark’s deaths.
